title: In May 2026, Egyptian billionaire and Aston Villa co-owner Nassef Sawiris celebr...
content:
In May 2026, Egyptian billionaire and Aston Villa co-owner Nassef Sawiris celebrated Aston Villa's UEFA Europa League triumph, marking the club's first European title since 1982.

Sports Most Valuable Arab Teams At FIFA World Cup 2026 The FIFA World Cup 2026 will be the largest edition in its 96-year history and features eight Arab national teams for the first time. Split evenly between Africa and Asia, the eight teams boast a combined market value of $1.1 billion as of May 5, 2026, according to the estimated transfer values of their players from football website Transfermarkt. Here are the top five by market value.

Comfi Co-founders Investments The Biggest Arab Sovereign Wealth Deals This Year Just seven Gulf sovereign wealth funds from the UAE, Saudi Arabia, Qatar and Kuwait accounted for 43% of all state-owned investment capital globally in 2025, according to Global SWF, and their growth shows no signs of slowing. Despite geopolitical tension, Arab SWFs are pushing harder with several notable deals in the first four months of 2026.	15

Stock Markets How GCC’s 7 Key Indices Performed Amid Tensions When regional tensions erupted in late February 2026, few investors expected a clean outcome. Geopolitical shocks typically trigger flight-to-safety selling across emerging markets, and the Gulf was no exception. Here’s how the GCC’s main indices performed from the end of February through April 2026.

Top 25 THE 250 GREATEST 1. Oprah Winfrey,72 Born to a teen mother, Winfrey grew up 18	on a rural Mississippi farm without indoor plumbing. At 14 she gave birth to a son, who died soon thereafter. Thanks to a federal SELF-MADE program, she attended a rich suburban school where she discovered a knack for public speaking and debate, which earned her a part-time radio gig and, later, a scholarship to Tennessee State University. In 1984 she took over a struggling morning talk show in Chicago and eventually turned AMERICANS	it into a national media brand. 2. Harold Hamm,80 Edited ByALEX KNAPPandLUISA KROLL, FORBES STAFF The 13th child of Oklahoma sharecroppers, Reported ByJESSICA JACOLBEandCHASE PETERSON-WITHORN Hamm’s earliest memory is picking • • •	tomatoes with his bare feet in the red Oklahoma dirt.
